title: oxalis laciniata var. pubescens
intro: Oxalis laciniata var. pubescens, also known as the hairy wood-sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the wood sorrel family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as a diuretic. Oxalis laciniata var. pubescens can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its leaves and stems can be dried and used as a flavoring agent or in traditional remedies.
